name: Fentanyl
executiveSummary: General opioid anesthetic with a mechanism of action similar to morphine. Adjunct to general anesthesia.
indications:
## Main indications

General opioid anesthetic with a mechanism of action similar to morphine. Adjunct to general anesthesia.

> Adjust according to institutional protocol and medical prescription.

pediatrico.dose: 1–12 years: 1–2 µg/kg/dose; >12 years and adults: 0.5–1 µg/kg/dose. Endotracheal intubation: 5–10 µg/kg. Patients on mechanical ventilation: continuous infusion: <50 kg: 1 µg/kg/h, ≥50 kg: 50 µg/h.
pediatrico.notes: SPECIALIST USE ONLY. Protect from light if administered as a pure drip. Bolus: administer over 3–5 min. Overdose may be treated with naloxone. Analgesic equivalence: 1 µg fentanyl = 0.1 mg morphine. See practical guide for analgosedation management and weaning in intermediate and moderate care units.

neonatal.dose: 0.5–2 mcg/kg bolus; infusion per NICU pain scale.

stability:
## General

- Dilution 24 h per package insert.

## Pediatric guide

- 24 h once diluted.
adverseEffects:
## Adverse effects

Apnea, bradycardia, rigid or wooden chest. See opioid analgesics section.
